one_line_description: All-in-one CRM platform for marketing, sales, service, and content, with a free tier and extensive automation features.
auth_summary: HubSpot APIs support exactly two methods: OAuth 2.0 (required for public/multi-account apps and Marketplace listings) and private app access tokens for single-account integrations; both are sent as Authorization: Bearer headers. Legacy API keys were retired in 2022.
access_notes: Fully self-serve: create a free HubSpot CRM account and generate a private app access token in settings, or create a free developer account to register an OAuth app. No paid plan or approval needed for basic API access, though some endpoints require paid Hub tiers.
api_surface_summary: Broad REST API covering CRM objects (contacts, companies, deals, tickets, custom objects), engagements/activities, CRM search, marketing, CMS, conversations, and more; now on a date-based versioning scheme (e.g., /crm/objects/2026-03/contacts).
agent_notes: Cleanest case of the two: self-serve free account, two well-documented auth methods, official remote MCP server (OAuth with PKCE) plus a local developer MCP server. Caveats: OAuth access tokens are short-lived (~30 min, refresh-token based), rate limits vary by plan, and some API areas (e.g., certain marketing/CMS features) are gated behind paid Hub tiers. HubSpot is migrating API reference to a new date-based versioning scheme, so endpoint paths in older docs differ from the latest reference.
